title: GIS & Spatial Analysis
description: The GIS module provides geospatial analysis capabilities that enable researchers to explore the geographic distribution of clinical events, disease burden, and social determinants of health. The GIS Explorer, contextual layers, and spatial analysis tools support population health research and epidemiological investigations.
tips: Overlay contextual layers such as socioeconomic indicators or environmental exposures onto patient distribution maps to investigate social determinants of health.
Use spatial analysis tools to identify geographic clustering of disease events and generate hypotheses for epidemiological investigation.
Ensure patient location data is appropriately de-identified or aggregated before visualizing in the GIS Explorer to maintain privacy compliance.
Combine GIS findings with cohort-level characterization data to enrich geographic analyses with clinical context.
Export spatial analysis outputs in standard formats for integration with external GIS tools or regulatory submissions.
